items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` **Triggered by:** commit 4773e9c11c70a78dba68eadc75d9af2820b98ad3 (PR #58068) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed A new `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` library was added (1.0.0-beta.1) providing declarative infrastructure provisioning for Azure Resource Graph shared queries. The library exposes `ResourceGraphQuery` and a `ResultKind` enum. A working snippet for creating a shared query is present in both the README and test file. ## Gaps Found - **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** ŌĆö no guidance on how authentication is handled in provisioning libraries (typically delegated to `azd` / Azure CLI; the library itself does not authenticate) - **`Next steps` section is empty** ŌĆö no links to Azure Resource Graph documentation, quickstarts, or related Azure.Provisioning.* libraries - **`Key concepts` section** doesn't document the `FromExisting` static method pattern, which is a core concept for all Azure.Provisioning.* libraries (allows referencing already-provisioned resources by Bicep identifier) - **`ResultKind` enum** (read-only output property on `ResourceGraphQuery`) is not mentioned or explained anywhere in the README
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` **1a. Fill in `Authenticate the Client` section** (after line 19, before `## Key concepts`): Replace: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client ## Key concepts ``` With: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client No authentication is required to define your infrastructure using this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph` generates Bicep templates that are deployed via the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) Authentication to Azure happens at deployment time through `azd` or the Azure CLI, not through this library. ## Key concepts ``` **1b. Expand `Key concepts` section** ŌĆö add the `FromExisting` pattern and `ResultKind` explanation. Replace: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates. ``` With: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates. ### ResourceGraphQuery `ResourceGraphQuery` represents an [Azure Resource Graph shared query]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Key writable properties include: | Property | Description | |----------|-------------| | `Query` | The KQL query string to execute against Azure Resource Graph | | `Description` | Human-readable description of the query | | `ETag` | Optimistic concurrency token; omit to always overwrite | | `Tags` | Freeform resource tags | The following properties are read-only output values set by Azure after deployment: | Property | Description | |----------|-------------| | `ResultKind` | Enum indicating the type of graph query result (`Basic` or other variants) | | `ModifiedOn` | UTC timestamp of the last modification | | `Id` | Full Azure resource identifier | ### Referencing existing resources Use the static `FromExisting` method to reference a Resource Graph shared query that has already been provisioned, rather than creating a new one: ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); ``` ``` **1c. Fill in `Next steps` section** ŌĆö replace the empty section: ```markdown ## Next steps - Learn more about [Azure Resource Graph]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Explore [Azure Resource Graph query samples]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - Get started with [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((aka.ms/redacted) to deploy your infrastructure - Browse related [`Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries]((www.nuget.org/redacted) for other Azure services ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **2a.** Add a `FromExisting` snippet to the test file at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/tests/BasicResourceGraphTests.cs`. Add a new test method to `BasicResourceGraphTests`: ```csharp [Test] public void ReferenceExistingResourceGraphQuery() { Infrastructure infra = new(); #region Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ResourceGraphQuery existingQuery = ResourceGraphQuery.FromExisting(nameof(existingQuery)); #endregion infra.Add(existingQuery); } ``` **2b.** Add the snippet placeholder in the README under the `### Referencing existing resources` subsection (see Step 1b above): ````markdown ```C# Snippet:ResourceGraphQueryFromExisting ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/README.md` currently has all required sections, but two are empty: | Section | Status | |---------|--------| | Getting started ŌåÆ Install the package | Ō£ģ Complete | | Getting started ŌåÆ Prerequisites | Ō£ģ Complete | | Getting started ŌåÆ Authenticate the Client | ŌØī Empty ŌĆö add content from Step 1a | | Key concepts | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Minimal ŌĆö expand with Step 1b | | Examples | Ō£ģ Has snippet | | Troubleshooting | Ō£ģ Complete | | Next steps | ŌØī Empty ŌĆö add content from Step 1c | | Contributing | Ō£ģ Complete | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcegraph` ŌĆö injects snippet code from test `#region` blocks into README placeholders; verify README diff shows the `FromExisting` snippet populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcegraph` ŌĆö no public API changes expected, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcegraph/Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ResourceGraph] Docs: Fill in empty sections and add key concepts for Azure.Provisioning.ResourceGraph
